close_reason: Implemented SHARD_INDEX section with mutation splitting, stable sort, and shard index building
description:
## Summary

Add SHARD_INDEX section to transaction wire format so Demux can split the log stream by shard without re-computing shard tags.

## Design

**New section**: SHARD_INDEX (tag 0x05)
- Payload: `[entry_count: varint, (tag: varint, count: varint)...]`
- Offset implied (mutations contiguous after stable sort)

**Mutation ordering**: Stable sort by shard tag before encoding
- Groups mutations by shard
- Preserves relative order within shard (critical for correctness)
- Cross-shard reordering safe (disjoint keyspaces)

**Cross-shard clear_range**: Split into clamped sub-ranges
- `CLEAR_RANGE "a" to "z"` spanning shards 0,1 becomes:
  - Shard 0: `CLEAR_RANGE "a" to "m"` (clamped)
  - Shard 1: `CLEAR_RANGE "m" to "z"` (clamped)

## Files to Modify

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| transaction.ex | Add SHARD_INDEX section, varint helpers, encode/decode |
| finalization.ex | Split mutations by shard, stable sort, build index |
| shard_router.ex | Add `lookup_shards_with_ranges/3` for boundaries |

## Data Flow

```
finalization: build_transactions_for_logs()
  ├── Split mutations by shard (clamp cross-shard ranges)
  ├── Stable sort by shard tag
  ├── Build shard index [(tag, count), ...]
  └── Encode with SHARD_INDEX section
        └── Demux reads index, splits by shard
```
title: Transaction Wire Format with Shard Tagging
